food_name: Fried chicken (2 pc) with fries and coleslaw
aliases: Fried chicken (2 pc) with fries and coleslaw

english: Fried chicken (2 pc) with fries and coleslaw

serving.description: 1 set meal (2 pieces fried chicken, medium fries, coleslaw)
serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving consists of two pieces of fried chicken, a medium portion of french fries, and a side of coleslaw.

nutrient_source.title: HPB Energy and Nutrient Composition of Food
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient data directly from HPB for 'Fried chicken (2 pc) with fries and coleslaw'.

gi_source.title: International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI for french fries (75) used as the primary carbohydrate component. Fried chicken and coleslaw have lower GI but fries dominate the overall glycemic response. Serving size of 300g assumed for GL calculation.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (441mg/100g) and low potassium.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit intake due to high sodium. Request reduced salt on fries and in chicken marinade, and opt for less mayonnaise in coleslaw.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index from deep-fried potato fries and low dietary fibre.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Diabetic individuals should avoid this meal due to the high glycemic load from fries. Substitute fries with a larger portion of salad or coleslaw (without sweet dressing) to improve glycemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (3g/100g), trans fat (0.3g/100g), and sodium content.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This meal is not suitable for individuals with cardiovascular disease due to high saturated fat, trans fat, and sodium. Opt for grilled chicken and a side salad instead of fried components.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fried chicken (2 pc) with fries and coleslaw is a popular Western set meal. It typically consists of two pieces of deep-fried chicken, a medium serving of deep-fried french fries, and a side of coleslaw made with shredded cabbage and carrots in a mayonnaise-based dressing. The chicken is crispy on the outside and juicy on the inside, while the fries are salty and starchy. The coleslaw offers a creamy, slightly tangy contrast. Nutritionally, this meal is high in energy, fat (including saturated and trans fats), and sodium, with a high glycemic index primarily due to the fries. It is generally considered unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease due to its nutrient profile.

confidence_reason: Nutrient data is directly from HPB Singapore for the exact food item. Health suitability and GI are assessed based on the known composition and established clinical guidelines.
notes: Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, potassium, cholesterol, and various vitamins/minerals were estimated based on typical values for fried chicken, french fries, and coleslaw from USDA FoodData Central, as HPB data was less granular for these specific nutrients. Trans fat at 0.3g/100g is a critical factor for rheumatoid arthritis.
